class.name: GameInputBinding
class.brief_description: One row in a [GameInputActionMap] mapping a GameInput source onto a Godot action.
class.description: [GameInputBinding] is an inspector-friendly [Resource] that connects a single GameInput source — either a button or an axis identified by [member source] — to a named action in Godot's [code]InputMap[/code]. [GameInputMapper] iterates over a [GameInputActionMap]'s bindings every frame and calls [code]Input.action_press()[/code] / [code]Input.action_release()[/code] based on the resolved device's reading, so the rest of the game can keep using standard [code]Input.is_action_pressed("jump")[/code] checks. When [member is_axis] is [code]false[/code] the binding tracks the source's bit in the buttons mask and emits press / release on those edges with [code]strength = 1.0[/code]. When [member is_axis] is [code]true[/code], the raw axis value is read, optionally inverted via [member axis_invert], a [member deadzone] is subtracted, the remainder is rescaled into [code][0, 1][/code], and the binding is considered "pressed" when the post-invert axis is positive and its absolute value exceeds [member axis_threshold]. The press strength sent to [code]Input.action_press[/code] is the post-deadzone magnitude. Bindings emit [code]changed[/code] (inherited from [Resource]) whenever any of their fields are modified, so editor inspectors and dependent resources update automatically.

method.name: get_action
method.description: Returns the action name this binding drives. See [member action].

method.name: set_action
method.description: Sets the action name this binding drives. See [member action].

method.name: get_source
method.description: Returns the [enum GameInputDevice.Source] id this binding listens to. See [member source].

method.name: set_source
method.description: Sets the [enum GameInputDevice.Source] id this binding listens to. See [member source].

method.name: get_is_axis
method.description: Returns whether this binding is interpreted as an axis. See [member is_axis].

method.name: set_is_axis
method.description: Sets whether this binding is interpreted as an axis. See [member is_axis].

method.name: get_axis_threshold
method.description: Returns the threshold above which an axis is considered "pressed". See [member axis_threshold].

method.name: set_axis_threshold
method.description: Sets the axis-as-button threshold. The value is clamped to [code][0.0, 1.0][/code]. See [member axis_threshold].

method.name: get_axis_invert
method.description: Returns whether the axis value is negated before deadzone / threshold logic runs. See [member axis_invert].

method.name: set_axis_invert
method.description: Sets whether the axis value is negated before deadzone / threshold logic runs. See [member axis_invert].

method.name: get_deadzone
method.description: Returns the deadzone subtracted from the absolute axis value before press evaluation. See [member deadzone].

method.name: set_deadzone
method.description: Sets the deadzone subtracted from the absolute axis value before press evaluation. The value is clamped to [code][0.0, 1.0][/code]. See [member deadzone].

members.member: Name of the action emitted via [code]Input.action_press[/code] / [code]Input.action_release[/code]. Must already exist in the project's [code]InputMap[/code]; [GameInputMapper] warns once per missing action and then ignores the binding.

The GameInput input this binding listens to. Accepts any [enum GameInputDevice.Source] value — buttons in [code][0, 13][/code] and axes in [code][100, 105][/code]. Defaults to [constant GameInputDevice.SRC_BTN_A].

When [code]false[/code], the binding treats [member source] as a button and emits press / release on edges of the buttons mask. When [code]true[/code], [member source] is treated as an axis and [member deadzone], [member axis_invert], and [member axis_threshold] all participate.

Absolute axis magnitude above which an axis-bound binding is considered "pressed". Only used when [member is_axis] is [code]true[/code]. Clamped to [code][0.0, 1.0][/code]. The press also requires the post-invert axis value to be positive, so a single bidirectional axis can be split into two opposing bindings via [member axis_invert].
When [code]true[/code], the raw axis value is negated before deadzone and threshold logic runs. Use this to bind, for example, "stick down" on a Y axis whose positive direction is up.

Deadzone applied to the absolute axis value before press evaluation. The remaining magnitude is rescaled into [code][0, 1][/code] and used as the press strength. Clamped to [code][0.0, 1.0][/code]. Only used when [member is_axis] is [code]true[/code].
